deep_dive.title: Understanding and Mitigating the Video-Action Generalization Gap via Temporal Ratio
deep_dive.abstract: Generative video foundation models exhibit strong compositional priors, yet world-action models (WAMs) and video-action models (VAMs) often lose these priors after finetuning on robotic action data. We refer to this discrepancy as the video-action generalization gap. In this paper, we systematically investigate this gap by evaluating a comprehensive design space of VAMs, demonstrating that standard design choices yield no emergent explanation pattern. To explain this behavior, we introduce the Temporal Ratio (TR), an attention-based measure of how strongly the action head relies on future latent rollouts relative to the anchored current frame. TR has two key properties: first, a model's structural reliance on future-predictive latents, measured via TR, acts as a predictor of its compositional generalization capacity; second, it natively fluctuates based on task phase, shifting attention to future frames during planning and reverting to the present frame for precise manipulation. Finally, based on these findings, we propose an inference-time adaptive guidance method, which exploits this intrinsic feature attention pattern to dynamically amplify compositional video conditioning signals precisely when the policy relies on future rollouts. Evaluated on the LIBERO benchmark and real-world tasks, our approach mitigates the OOD-ID compositional generalization gap. More details: https://umishra.me/temporal-ratio/
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: analyzes video-action generalization gap in world-action models with temporal ratio
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 分析世界动作模型中的视频-动作泛化差距并提出时间比指标
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 分析世界动作模型中的视频-动作泛化差距并提出时间比指标
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 分析世界动作模型中的视频-动作泛化差距并提出时间比指标
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper investigates the video-action generalization gap in world-action models, introduces Temporal Ratio to measure reliance on future latents, and provides insights for designing better models.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 世界动作模型在微调后常损失预训练的视频先验，导致泛化差距。本文系统评估了多种设计空间，并引入时间比（Temporal Ratio）度量模型对未来潜在表示的依赖程度。实验表明，时间比与泛化性能强相关。该工作为改进基于世界模型的强化学习提供了重要指导。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 世界动作模型在微调后常损失预训练的视频先验，导致泛化差距。本文系统评估了多种设计空间，并引入时间比（Temporal Ratio）度量模型对未来潜在表示的依赖程度。实验表明，时间比与泛化性能强相关。该工作为改进基于世界模型的强化学习提供了重要指导。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: reinforcement learning with learned world dynamics

deep_dive.title: LEEVLA: Seeing What Matters in Latent Environment Evolution for Vision-Language-Action
deep_dive.abstract: Vision-language-action (VLA) models aim to map multimodal inputs to robot actions. However, most existing approaches struggle to cover complex dynamic scenarios due to treating all visual tokens uniformly and reasoning with human-selected factors, which lack mechanisms to emphasize task-critical evidence and ignore underlying factors. To address this issue, we propose LEEVLA, a VLA architecture for seeing what matters in Latent Environment Evolution that explicitly guides the model toward informative regions while preserving the structured evolution of latent world representations. To identify salient and instruction-relevant regions, we introduce drift-guided dynamic prioritization (DGDP), which combines dynamic position prioritization (DPP) with semantic drift guidance (SDG) to guide the VLA agent where to attend during training. On top of this, we introduce structured feature flow generation (SFFG), which models how these prioritized features should evolve in latent space via prototype-to-periphery (P2P) prediction, and a mutual-neighborhood contrastive (MC) loss to maintain topological consistency among neighborhoods. Together, DGDP and SFFG form a task-aware "where-how" training framework. Extensive experiments on VLA benchmarks show that LEEVLA consistently outperforms prior methods, confirming that explicit task-evidence guidance and structured latent reasoning are both crucial for scalable VLA. Our code is available at https://github.com/LyuQi127/LEEVLA.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: latent environment evolution for world model in VLA
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于视觉-语言-动作模型的潜环境演化世界模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于视觉-语言-动作模型的潜环境演化世界模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于视觉-语言-动作模型的潜环境演化世界模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Explicitly models latent world evolution and guides attention to informative regions for robust VLA policy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有VLA模型均匀处理视觉令牌，缺乏强调任务关键信息的机制，难以应对复杂动态场景。LEEVLA提出潜环境演化架构，引入漂移引导动态优先级（DGDP）突出任务相关区域，同时保持潜世界表示的结构化演化。该方法在多种机器人操作任务中显著提升鲁棒性和泛化能力，展示了潜世界模型在VLA中的有效应用。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有VLA模型均匀处理视觉令牌，缺乏强调任务关键信息的机制，难以应对复杂动态场景。LEEVLA提出潜环境演化架构，引入漂移引导动态优先级（DGDP）突出任务相关区域，同时保持潜世界表示的结构化演化。该方法在多种机器人操作任务中显著提升鲁棒性和泛化能力，展示了潜世界模型在VLA中的有效应用。

quick_skim.title: TypeGo: An OS Runtime for Embodied Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) can plan behavior for embodied agents from natural language, but treating the LLM as a request/response oracle on the critical path is fundamentally at odds with real-time control and concurrent goals. We argue for an operating-system-style runtime for embodied agents, and instantiate this idea in an early prototype, TypeGo. TypeGo structures LLM-based planning as asynchronous loops at multiple timescales that overlap with execution, and manages the agent's physical body like an OS manages hardware: the Skill Kernel arbitrates typed physical subsystems among concurrent per-task processes, a scheduler preempts them and resumes or replaces each by source, and speculative skill streaming hides LLM latency behind ongoing motion, while a fast first-action path yields visible feedback within a second. Users program behavior through natural language prescriptions that TypeGo dispatches to the LLM-based planners or compiles into low-latency interrupt handlers. Our prototype of Kalos, a Unitree Go2 quadruped, provides preliminary evidence for the design: in our current task suite, it cuts per-step delay by 50% over step-by-step planning and time-to-first-action by 73% over monolithic planning, while admitting concurrent tasks at low scheduling overhead.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: OS runtime for embodied agents with LLM-based planning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 为具身智能体设计操作系统运行时，基于LLM的规划
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 为具身智能体设计操作系统运行时，基于LLM的规划
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 为具身智能体设计操作系统运行时，基于LLM的规划
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes TypeGo, an OS-like runtime for embodied agents that manages LLM-based planning as asynchronous loops for real-time control.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 具身智能体需要实时并发控制，但LLM的请求响应模式不适合。TypeGo提出操作系统式运行时，将LLM规划组织为异步多时间尺度循环，通过技能内核仲裁物理子系统、调度器管理进程，并采用推测性技能流隐藏延迟。原型验证了该方法能有效支持实时具身任务，为LLM在机器人中的应用提供了新架构。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 具身智能体需要实时并发控制，但LLM的请求响应模式不适合。TypeGo提出操作系统式运行时，将LLM规划组织为异步多时间尺度循环，通过技能内核仲裁物理子系统、调度器管理进程，并采用推测性技能流隐藏延迟。原型验证了该方法能有效支持实时具身任务，为LLM在机器人中的应用提供了新架构。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: artificial intelligence integrated with physical agents and sensors

quick_skim.title: Safe Reinforcement Learning using Ideas from Model Predictive Control
quick_skim.abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) enables the synthesis of control policies directly from data, making it highly appealing for complex cyber-physical systems (CPSs) and robotics. A persistent challenge, however, is ensuring strict, hard safety constraints during the active learning phase. In real-world physical systems, violating mechanical limits can cause irreversible damage, necessitating that exploration remains strictly within safe operational regions. We propose a generalized framework that combines the adaptive, high-performance nature of deep reinforcement learning (DRL) with the formal safety guarantees of model predictive control (MPC). Using a mathematical model of the system dynamics, offline MPC computations define a feasible state-action space, representing all safe combinations of system states and control inputs that guarantee constraint satisfaction. During training and deployment, the RL agent's instantaneous actions are projected onto this globally verified feasible set via a safety filter. We systematically evaluate our generalized approach on a non-linear 1-DoF laboratory testbed, demonstrating successful exploration and stable policy convergence on physical hardware.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses a mathematical model of system dynamics for safe RL
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用系统动力学数学模型进行安全强化学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用系统动力学数学模型进行安全强化学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用系统动力学数学模型进行安全强化学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a framework combining deep RL with MPC to enforce safety constraints using a learned dynamics model, relevant to model-based RL.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对强化学习在线探索中的安全约束问题，提出将深度强化学习与模型预测控制结合，利用系统动力学模型离线计算可行状态动作空间。实验表明该方法在保证安全的同时保持了高性能。这项工作为安全强化学习提供了一种通用框架，直接支持基于模型的学习。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对强化学习在线探索中的安全约束问题，提出将深度强化学习与模型预测控制结合，利用系统动力学模型离线计算可行状态动作空间。实验表明该方法在保证安全的同时保持了高性能。这项工作为安全强化学习提供了一种通用框架，直接支持基于模型的学习。

quick_skim.title: Harness VLA: Steering Frozen VLAs into Reliable Manipulation Primitives via Memory-Guided Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Language-conditioned manipulation requires both precise contact-rich control and robust reasoning over language, scenes, and long horizons. End-to-end Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models provide strong local visuomotor skills, but they are trained on in-distribution task trajectories and often fail under deployment perturbations such as semantic retargeting, goal re-binding, spatial-layout shifts, and unstable local contacts. LLM coding agents provide complementary semantic and compositional reasoning, but purely analytic primitives struggle with irregular grasping, constrained placement, and articulated-object interaction. We present Harness VLA, a memory-augmented agentic framework that exposes a frozen VLA as a retryable contact-rich primitive and composes it with a small fixed library of analytic primitives for grounding, staging, transport, navigation, and release. Rather than expanding the skill library, the harness learns the operating range of these fixed primitives from task-specific execution traces, global success rules, and failure models. By lifting semantic re-grounding, non-contact execution, and VLA re-staging to the planner while reserving the frozen VLA for local contact-rich phases, Harness VLA extends pretrained VLAs beyond their original trajectory distribution without finetuning. Across perturbed tabletop, household kitchen, and clean-to-randomized bimanual manipulation, Harness VLA improves over the strongest relevant baselines by 38.6 and 25.4 percentage points on LIBERO-Pro and RoboCasa365, respectively, and reaches 58.4% on RoboTwin C2R.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: imitation learning for robotic manipulation with frozen VLA models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用冻结VLA模型的机器人操作模仿学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用冻结VLA模型的机器人操作模仿学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用冻结VLA模型的机器人操作模仿学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Harness VLA steers frozen Vision-Language-Action models into reliable manipulation primitives via memory-augmented agents, improving robustness under distribution shifts.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 端到端VLA模型在部署扰动下易失败，LLM编码代理缺乏精细操作技能。Harness VLA提出记忆增强代理框架，将冻结VLA暴露为可重试协作基元，通过语义重定向、空间布局变化等压力测试，实现鲁棒的语言条件操作。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 端到端VLA模型在部署扰动下易失败，LLM编码代理缺乏精细操作技能。Harness VLA提出记忆增强代理框架，将冻结VLA暴露为可重试协作基元，通过语义重定向、空间布局变化等压力测试，实现鲁棒的语言条件操作。
